feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue with Finding Entities in SAP Standard API for Equipment MarkForDeletion Functionality
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently working on utilizing the SAP Standard API for equipment management, specifically to implement the "MarkForDeletion" functionality. I referred to the API details on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where I found the following endpoints related to equipment deletion:

  • POST /ResetEQUIPFromInactive
  • POST /SetEQUIPToInactive
  • POST /SetEQUIPToMarkedForDeletion

I have attached a snapshot from the Business Accelerator Hub showing these endpoints for reference (Snapshot 1).

However, when I checked the available entity sets in the SAP Gateway Client using transaction code /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE for the service /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_EQUIPMENT/, I could not find the corresponding entities or operations for the above endpoints. The entity sets I found are limited to:

  • EQUIPClassCharacteristicValue
  • EQUIPMENT
  • EQUIPMENTClass
  • EQUIPMENTClassCharacteristic
  • EQUIPMENTLONGTEXT
  • EQUIPMENTPARTNER
  • EQUIPMENTWARRANTY

I have attached a snapshot from the SAP Gateway Client showing the available entity sets (Snapshot 2).

My questions are:

  1. Where can I find the detailed implementation or metadata for the SetEQUIPToMarkedForDeletion functionality in the SAP system?
  2. Are these endpoints (ResetEQUIPFromInactive, SetEQUIPToInactive, SetEQUIPToMarkedForDeletion) part of a different service or require additional configuration to be visible in the Gateway Client?
  3. How can I proceed with using the "MarkForDeletion" functionality for equipment via the API?

We are keen on using this functionality for our project, so any guidance on how to locate or enable these operations would be greatly appreciated.

Looking forward to your insights!

Attachments:

  • Snapshot 1: Business Accelerator Hub API Endpoints
  • Snapshot 2: SAP Gateway Client Entity Sets

Thank you,

entry.title: Large Volume of Data Replication – SAP S/4HANA to SAP Commerce Cloud (Hybris): Hotfolders vs. SCPI?
entry.content: For a large volume of product master data — approximately 5 million records — with daily inventory and pricing updates, we are evaluating for the best replication/integration (SAP to Hybris) approach in terms of performance, throughput, and resource utilization. In this case, which option would be more suitable: via Hotfolders (Azure Storage) or SAP Cloud Platform Integration (SCPI)?

entry.title: CAP application with multiple databases (HANA + PostgreSQL)?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Is it possible to configure a SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) application to use more than one database?

For example:

  • For production, I want to have a primary database such as SAP HANA.

  • But I also want to be able to connect to a secondary database, like PostgreSQL, from within the same CAP application.

If this is possible, could you please provide an example of how the package.json configuration would look?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Logout functionality SAP CAP application deployed in Cloud Foundry using Managed approuter
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have built a SAP CAP application with UI5 and used managed approuter. The application is deployed in Cloud Foundry and I can access it through HTML5 applications. This application uses XSUAA authentication. 

I want to implement logout functionality in this application for the user. I have read many blogs and I'm unable to implement it. I have done things like making changes in xs-app.json file to include the logout parameter as described in the link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/logout

On the logout button in my app, i put the controller logic to 

window.location.replace("/my/logout").

After this when I click on logout, it takes me to a URL:
"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap-ui-logout-url" and when i click the back button, it again goes directly into the application logged in as the previous user, which means that it is not deleting the session maybe.

I even followed the question:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/logout-sap-ui5-app-cloud-foundry-sample-code/qaq-p/12248689

(Yes, I gave relative path in the controller script like my/logout as I am using managed approuter)

But when i do this, i get an internal server error after clicking on logout.

On checking the error logs of the app, I see the error as:
GET request to /74cf1c07-1827-49d2-9bc9-90ba4d9f6781.capleavemanagementappservice.leavemanagement-1.0.0/my/logout completed with status 500 request method (GET) does not match logout configuration logoutMethod (POST)


Does anybody know how can I implement a logout functionality in my application?

Thanks,
Raihan

entry.title: Call a destination(OAuth2UserTokenExchange) from SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I am trying to call a destination from SAP Build Process Automation.

The destination is configured with authorization OAuth2UserTokenExchange.

I get error while using the "Call web service with destination" activity and also while configuring the actions.

Please look at the attached screenshots.

Kindly help me on how to resolve this.

Regards

entry.title: Best Approach to Trigger SAP CPI Interface Periodically from S/4HANA with Dynamic Plant/S.Loc
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement where a SAP CPI interface needs to be triggered once or twice daily. This interface performs the following:

--Accepts Plant and Storage Location as input.

--Fetches product data using an OData API in S/4HANA and some additional info from other APIs.

--Sends the final data to a target system.

What we initially considered:

Timer-based iFlow in CPI, with plant and storage location maintained as externalized parameters in a content modifier.

----Rejected by the team due to manual effort involved in changing parameters each time we want to trigger for different plant/storage location.

SAP BTP Job Scheduler: Allows periodic triggering and could call a CPI endpoint with required parameters. Currently in discussion. If selected, the job would pass Plant and Storage Location as part of the payload to CPI.

S/4HANA-triggered interface via SOAP/IDoc: S/4 can periodically send data (plant and storage location) via a custom ABAP job, which triggers a CPI endpoint using SOAP or sends an IDoc. This seems promising, but I would like to know what’s more standard/recommended.

Constraints:

We cannot use custom Z-tables in S/4HANA to manage or store configuration.

CPI as a public API for third-party to call isn’t suitable, as the source system must be S/4 or CI.

Event Mesh is not applicable here, since there is no real-time change or event-based trigger. It’s purely a periodic push.

My Questions:

1) Between BTP Job Scheduler and S/4HANA SOAP/IDoc push, which would be more recommended and scalable?

2) Are there any other better or more standard options to periodically trigger a CPI interface with dynamic parameters like plant and storage location?

entry.title: BTP Trial IAS Provisioning Issue
entry.content: “My BTP trial global account ID is 58dc44e9trial in us10. Identity Authentication token URL exists, but the admin UI and SAML metadata both return errors (Uh oh, 404). I'm unable to establish trust to subscribe to SAP Build Work Zone. Please advise.”

entry.title: User does not have sufficient privileges in Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi

I have created a process automation like a bellow example 
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-academy-salesorder.html

here it is working fine, and workflow is triggering while creating the instance from Process and Workflow>> Start New instance and Close. 

But when i created the Build App for the API like bellow:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

But here it is giving error at the time for workflow instance 

"responseText": "{\"error\":{\"code\":\"bpm.workflowruntime.rest.user.not.sufficient.privileges\",\"message\":\"User does not have sufficient privileges.\"}}"

I have assign the role also, But still giving error. Can any one help me please....

entry.title: Custom logback.xml Deployment on SAP BTP (tomee7)
entry.content:
I am currently deploying a Java application to the SAP BTP environment, using the following setup:

  • TARGET_RUNTIME: tomee7
  • Deployment Type: Java WAR application

My application includes a custom logback.xml configuration for logging purposes, which is placed under src/main/resources. However, upon deployment, I noticed that the application seems to use the default logback.xml provided by the tomee7 runtime, rather than the one included in my application package.

I would appreciate your support in clarifying the following:

  1. Is it possible to override the default logback.xml used by the tomee7 runtime with a custom configuration bundled in my WAR file?
  2. If yes, could you please advise:
    • The correct placement of the logback.xml file within the WAR structure?
    • Any additional configuration required to ensure the runtime uses my custom file?
  3. If not, is there a supported mechanism to achieve custom logging configurations within the tomee7 runtime on BTP?

entry.title: Could not create integration suite in my trail BTP account.
entry.content: I created the integration suit before but today it was not working properly, could not add a new flow. deleted it but could not add it again

entry.title: Integration flows do not work in the Integration Suite Trial version.
entry.content:
Starting from June 9, 2025, the issue began.
In the SAP Trial version, I am unable to create integration flows. I also cannot edit or deploy integration flows after importing them. Overall, none of the integration flow-related features are working.

entry.title: Integration flows do not work in the Integration Suite Trial version.
entry.content:
Starting from June 9, 2025, the issue began.
In the SAP Trial version, I am unable to create integration flows. I also cannot edit or deploy integration flows after importing them. Overall, none of the integration flow-related features are working.

1. Integration flow Selcet or Edit


2. Integration flow deploy

3. Integration Suite Version

entry.title: My BTP Trail account Subscription failed for Integration suite
entry.content:
Hello, I couldn't add my integration suite application in my BTP sub account's. As I have Subscription Failed error.

I need some here:

entry.title: How to Identify Your SAP BTP Global Administrator in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Symptom: You don't know who the BTP Global Admin is.

Requirements: You need to have an S-user and the role of Super Administrator or Cloud Administrator assigned in SAP for Me. 

  • If you don't have an S-User, please check this page.
  • If you are unsure which role you have, please check this note. 

Resolution:

1) Open SAP for Me and log in.

2) Navigate to the "Dashboards" section and click "Systems & Provisioning".

3) Select "Systems".

4) Search for "Business Technology Platform".

5) Click the link provided in the Global Account Column.

6) A new page will open. Scroll down to find the "Global Account Administrators" card.

Image 1

Image 2

Source: 2669325 - How to add a user as a SAP Business Technology Platform Global Account administrator

entry.title: SAP Service & Asset Manager - Reading data from local SSAM database in Dynamic Forms
entry.content:
Hello!

We have developed custom pages in MDK, in SSAM, to represent custom forms from our client within a work order operation. We are now evaluating if we can move these custom pages to SAP Dynamic Forms. We have not used this functionality before.

We have several ListPickers and conditions between fields that rely on reading information stored in the local database. For example, dynamic listPicker that shows current meters serial numbers synchronized into the app, with a dynamic query option.

We have seen in the product documentation, the functionality "Database Services" that somehow could work. However, it is advertised that the functionality is not currently supported.

Is it possible to do so with Dynamic Forms? Also, is it possible to pre populate fields in the form with data stored in the SSAM database?

thank you in advance for your help!!
Best regards,
Gonzalo.

entry.title: Blog Post Still 'Awaiting Review' After 24 Hours – Request for Update
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community Team,

I submitted a blog post titled "Build and Deploy a Full-Stack SAP CAPM App with Fiori UI on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry" more than 24 hours ago. It's still showing as “Awaiting Review”, and the URL is not publicly accessible yet.

Could you please let me know the estimated time for moderation or if any action is required from my side?

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Madhan

entry.title: restore sap cloud connector connection Cloud To On-premise
entry.content:
Hello,

Can anyone tell me how to restore all the connections configured on the sap cloud connector (deleted by mistake): Cloud To On Premise.

Thanks & Best Regards

entry.title: Is there a BTP service or API to track user login/logout times and session duration?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a project in SAP BTP and would like to know if there is any built-in service or API that can provide information such as:

  • When a user logged in

  • When they logged out

  • How long they stayed logged in (session duration)

This would help us monitor usage patterns and possibly audit access times.

Is there a service (like Audit Log, Identity Authentication, or another BTP capability) that offers this kind of data? If so, how can it be accessed?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Subscription failed in BTP trail account
entry.content:
Hi,

I am getting the below failed message during Build process automation subscription. Please help on this?

Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

Thanks.

entry.title: Issue with Destination Visibility in Cloud Identity Services – Connectivity Plan Required or Workaro
entry.content:
We are currently facing an issue when attempting to create a Source System in the Identity Provisioning Service. Specifically, the destination we've configured in our SAP BTP subaccount isn't appearing as an option in Cloud Identity Services. Based on our research, it seems we may need to create a Cloud Identity Services Connectivity Plan within the same subaccount, as suggested by this community thread: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/no-destination-available-at-identity-provisioning-service/qaq-p/12609309.

We have been following this comprehensive guide to enable Joule capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-a-comprehensive-setup-guide/ba-p/13786453.

However, we encountered an error while trying to create this Connectivity Plan, matching the scenario described in SAP Knowledge Base Article 3462424: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3462424.

Could the absence of this Connectivity Plan indeed be the root cause of our issue, or is there an alternative workaround available that would allow the already-created BTP destination to appear within the Cloud Identity Services? Any insights or guidance from the community would be greatly appreciated.
